page: UpCloud GPU-32xCPU-384GB-2xL4 is a GPU-accelerated virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 32 shared vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding 12.0 GB of RAM per core. The server is equipped with two NVIDIA L4 GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing a total of 48 GB of VRAM.

This instance configuration does not include local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for operating system and data storage. It includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation paired with high-capacity system memory and dual professional-grade GPUs offers a resource-dense profile for workloads that require heavy parallel processing without needing dedicated physical CPU cores.

The hardware configuration is designed for GPU-accelerated workloads. Appropriate applications include machine learning inference, data science jobs, video transcoding, and parallel computing tasks that leverage the dual NVIDIA L4 GPUs and the large 384.0 GB memory footprint.
description: UpCloud GPU-32xCPU-384GB-2xL4 is a GPU-accelerated virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 32 shared vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory, providing 12.0 GB of RAM per core. The server is equipped with two NVIDIA L4 GPUs from the Ada Lovelace family, delivering a total of 48 GB of VRAM. No local storage is bundled with this instance, meaning storage must be provisioned externally. It includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. This configuration is designed for resource-intensive parallel processing tasks. The combination of high system memory and dual GPUs makes it suitable for workloads such as machine learning inference, data science, and video processing, offering a cost-effective, resource-dense option via its shared CPU allocation.
